description: Hand-curated corpus of cross-post pairs plus near-miss decoys. Used to measure FusedMomentDetector false-positive and false-negative rates against the spec acceptance criteria. Must expand to >= 100 with REAL cross-post pairs pulled from production feeds before v1.0 promote-to-App-Store. Design rule for negatives: every negative entry must be NEAR the positive class along at least one axis — same content/different author, identical content/different timing, short content under the confidence threshold, etc. Rejection by content mismatch alone tests nothing about the detector's decision boundaries. KNOWN GAPS (deferred to v1.x): same-author copy-paste reply patterns produce a false positive without parent-thread context. Withhold this corpus shape until a parent-thread signal lands.

examples.label: positive
examples.note: Identical text, well above the short-content threshold

masto.content: Just shipped a new version with much better cross-poster handling!

bsky.content: Just shipped a new version with much better cross-poster handling!

examples.label: positive
examples.note: Trailing hashtags differ between networks; content well above short-content threshold

masto.content: Sunset over the bay was absolutely spectacular tonight, glad I caught it

bsky.content: Sunset over the bay was absolutely spectacular tonight, glad I caught it #sunset #sf

examples.label: negative
examples.note: Same author, similar topic, different posts — should NOT fuse

masto.content: Loving this album, every track is a banger

bsky.content: Best album of the year imo, every single track works

examples.label: negative
examples.note: Identical content but different authors — should NOT fuse

masto.content: Happy Friday everyone, may your weekend be restful

bsky.content: Happy Friday everyone, may your weekend be restful

examples.label: negative
examples.note: Same content/author but posted hours apart — should NOT fuse (intentional repost, not a cross-post)

masto.content: Reminder: meetup at the usual spot starting at 7 pm tonight

bsky.content: Reminder: meetup at the usual spot starting at 7 pm tonight

examples.label: boundary-positive
examples.note: Content exactly above 20-char short-content threshold; should fuse at baseline 0.85

masto.content: Off to the airport at last

bsky.content: Off to the airport at last

examples.label: boundary-negative
examples.note: Content under 20-char short-content threshold; confidence 0.65 < 0.75 minConfidence — should NOT fuse

masto.content: tea time again

bsky.content: tea time again

examples.label: positive
examples.note: URLs differ only in trailing slash — ContentSignature must normalize

masto.content: Just read this great write-up: https://example.com/post

bsky.content: Just read this great write-up: https://example.com/post/

examples.label: positive
examples.note: URLs differ only in fragment — ContentSignature must strip #fragments

masto.content: Cracking section on dataflow in here: https://example.com/docs

bsky.content: Cracking section on dataflow in here: https://example.com/docs#dataflow

examples.label: positive
examples.note: URL trailing punctuation — one network keeps the period inside the URL, the other doesn't

masto.content: Found a beautiful walk along the coast: https://example.com/coast.

bsky.content: Found a beautiful walk along the coast: https://example.com/coast

examples.label: positive
examples.note: Trailing @mention differs (bare handle vs federated handle@host)

masto.content: Brilliant talk on cross-network thinking by @speaker@conference.tld

bsky.content: Brilliant talk on cross-network thinking by @speaker

examples.label: positive
examples.note: Mixed case differences; ContentSignature lowercases before compare

masto.content: Big News for the team — onboarding shipped today

bsky.content: Big news for the team — onboarding shipped today

examples.label: positive
examples.note: Whitespace differences: one side wraps with extra newlines, the other doesn't

masto.content:
Working through the migration today.
Takes time but worth it.

bsky.content: Working through the migration today. Takes time but worth it.

examples.label: positive
examples.note: Both trailing hashtag AND trailing mention differ — combined normalization

masto.content: Slowly rebuilding my home office, more shelves arriving soon @maker

bsky.content: Slowly rebuilding my home office, more shelves arriving soon #wfh

examples.label: boundary-positive
examples.note: Time gap just under 10-minute window (599s) — should still fuse

masto.content: Made it to the conference, looking forward to the keynote

bsky.content: Made it to the conference, looking forward to the keynote

examples.label: boundary-negative
examples.note: Time gap just over 10-minute window (601s) — should NOT fuse

masto.content: Lunch was decent, time to get back to work for the afternoon

bsky.content: Lunch was decent, time to get back to work for the afternoon

examples.label: negative
examples.note: Word-order rearrangement — same words, different sentence — should NOT fuse

masto.content: The cat sat on the warm sunny windowsill all afternoon

bsky.content: All afternoon the cat sat on the warm sunny windowsill

examples.label: negative
examples.note: Leading emoji differs — different content, should NOT fuse

masto.content: 🎉 Out of beta and into the world, thank you for testing

bsky.content: 🎊 Out of beta and into the world, thank you for testing

examples.label: positive
examples.note: Realistic crosspost with URL and longer text — typical happy-path entry

masto.content: Wrote up the design rationale for our merged-identity layer: https://example.com/merged-identity

bsky.content: Wrote up the design rationale for our merged-identity layer: https://example.com/merged-identity

examples.label: positive
examples.note: Long content with URL + trailing hashtag on one side only

masto.content: Spent the morning refactoring the timeline-buffer ringbuffer; pleasantly surprised by how much simpler it got after dropping the eviction-policy speculation https://example.com/notes

bsky.content: Spent the morning refactoring the timeline-buffer ringbuffer; pleasantly surprised by how much simpler it got after dropping the eviction-policy speculation https://example.com/notes #swift

examples.label: negative
examples.note: Short content right at the threshold — even with same author and tight timing, baseline minus shortContentPenalty falls below minConfidence; should NOT fuse

masto.content: weather is wild

bsky.content: weather is wild

examples.label: positive
examples.note: Emoji + sentence — Unicode in content must not affect fingerprinting

masto.content: Finally got the bloom animation right ✨ Took three rewrites but it's worth it

bsky.content: Finally got the bloom animation right ✨ Took three rewrites but it's worth it

examples.label: positive
examples.note: Trailing punctuation differs (period dropped on one side) — ContentSignature strips terminal punctuation

masto.content: Reading group meets Thursdays at 7 — Mastodon and Bluesky both welcome.

bsky.content: Reading group meets Thursdays at 7 — Mastodon and Bluesky both welcome

examples.label: positive
examples.note: Cross-poster prepends '@mention' on one side; ContentSignature strips leading mentions

masto.content: Big thanks to everyone who reviewed the early build last week — feedback was invaluable

bsky.content: @beta-testers.bsky.social Big thanks to everyone who reviewed the early build last week — feedback was invaluable

examples.label: positive
examples.note: Long-form, both sides identical, well above longContentBonus threshold

masto.content: After spending most of yesterday reading through old design notes, I think the answer for cross-network conversation stitching is to treat the conversation as the unit of identity, not the post. Posts are platform-specific by necessity; conversations are not. The hard part is convincing the data layer to agree.

bsky.content: After spending most of yesterday reading through old design notes, I think the answer for cross-network conversation stitching is to treat the conversation as the unit of identity, not the post. Posts are platform-specific by necessity; conversations are not. The hard part is convincing the data layer to agree.

examples.label: negative
examples.note: Looks like a quote of someone else's post — same wording, distinct author handles; should NOT fuse across users

masto.content: Reading: 'Native craft is the floor, not the goal.' — well said and worth pinning

bsky.content: Reading: 'Native craft is the floor, not the goal.' — well said and worth pinning

examples.label: negative
examples.note: Cross-poster within window but content meaningfully diverged (added a P.S.) — signatures differ

masto.content: Found a great new coffee spot down the street, will report back after a few visits

bsky.content: Found a great new coffee spot down the street, will report back after a few visits. Update: their drip is excellent

examples.label: boundary-positive
examples.note: Right at the 10-minute window boundary — must still fuse since the detector uses <= timeWindow

masto.content: Heading out for the afternoon, will catch up on replies after dinner

bsky.content: Heading out for the afternoon, will catch up on replies after dinner

examples.label: negative
examples.note: Just past the 10-minute window — same content, same author, but >600s apart, should NOT fuse

masto.content: Spent the afternoon at the art museum, the Sargent exhibit was magnificent

bsky.content: Spent the afternoon at the art museum, the Sargent exhibit was magnificent

examples.label: positive
examples.note: Whitespace difference (extra space) — fingerprint normalization should collapse

masto.content: Polished off another iteration of the keyboard-shortcut polish pass. Feels good.

bsky.content: Polished off another iteration  of the keyboard-shortcut polish pass. Feels good.

examples.label: positive
examples.note: Question-mark sentence; ensures detector handles non-statement content

masto.content: Anyone else find Mastodon's federated timeline weirdly comforting these days?

bsky.content: Anyone else find Mastodon's federated timeline weirdly comforting these days?

examples.label: negative
examples.note: URL appears in both sides but text otherwise diverges — should NOT fuse on a shared URL alone

masto.content: Bookmarking this excellent essay on cross-network identity: https://example.com/identity-essay

bsky.content: Filed this under: things I'll re-read next quarter. https://example.com/identity-essay

examples.label: positive
examples.note: Cross-poster reorders trailing tags; primary text identical

masto.content: Day 3 of the conference and the hallway track is somehow even better than the keynotes #conf2026 #indieweb

bsky.content: Day 3 of the conference and the hallway track is somehow even better than the keynotes #indieweb #conf2026

examples.label: negative
examples.note: Empty content on one side (perhaps an image-only Bluesky post); detector must never fuse on empty

masto.content: View from the office today
